name: Undertale
Description:
Welcome to UNDERTALE. In this RPG, you control a human who falls underground into the world of monsters. Now you must find your way out... or stay trapped forever.
((Healthy Dog's Warning: Game contains imagery that may be harmful to players with photosensitive epilepsy or similar condition.))
features:
Killing is unnecessary: negotiate out of danger using the unique battle system.
Time your attacks for extra damage, then dodge enemy attacks in a style reminiscent of top-down shooters.
Original art and soundtrack brimming with personality.
Soulful, character-rich story with an emphasis on humor.
Created mostly by one person.
Become friends with all of the bosses!
At least 5 dogs.
You can date a skeleton.
Hmmm... now there are 6 dogs...?
Maybe you won't want to date the skeleton.
I thought I found a 7th dog, but it was actually just the 3rd dog.
If you play this game, can you count the dogs for me...? I'm not good at it.
requirements:
Minimum requirements:
Operating System*: Windows XP, Vista, 7, 8, or 10
Memory: 2 GB RAM
Graphics card: 128MB
Storage space: Requires 200 MB of available space
Recommended configuration:
Operating System*: Windows XP, Vista, 7, or 10
Processor: 2GHz+
Memory: 3 GB RAM
Graphics card: 512MB
Storage space: Requires 200 MB of available space
Comment1:
My impressions of playing Undertale after paying the fare.

As a belated player, I finally sat down and finished Undertale, and only then did I understand why this old game is considered a masterpiece by so many. The simple pixel art style and straightforward bullet hell combat may seem retro and rudimentary, but they conceal the most delicate tenderness and profound thoughts.

Breaking away from the traditional RPG logic of leveling up by killing monsters and gaining strength through combat, UT presents a unique proposition of choice. In the journey through the underground world, every monster is not merely a minor obstacle; they possess their own obsessions, tenderness, and helplessness. You can choose to fight back, following the momentum to gain experience and power; or you can set aside hostility, forgive through action and compassion, and complete the journey as a humble being.

What struck me most was the game's powerful self-memory and its fourth-wall breaking design. It remembers every choice you make, and saving and loading are no longer just simple restart mechanisms, but rather mirrors reflecting your inner self. In the Mercy route, you encounter and accompany various characters, gradually understanding the sorrow and desires of the underworld, with the warm and healing ending striking a chord deep within. In contrast, the oppressive and backlash-filled Genocide route makes you truly realize that violence never comes without consequences.

The background music that runs throughout, the vivid and three-dimensional characters, the plot twists and turns, and the subtle foreshadowing details give this small underground world a strong sense of vitality. Even after many years, I wasn't deterred by the outdated gameplay; instead, I was moved by its pure and unique design.

Undertale is more than just a game; it gently tells players that kindness is never weakness, and choosing goodwill is the most precious ending to this adventure. This emotion that spans many years makes this belated ticket purchase all the more worthwhile.
Comment2: Undertale is ultimately a traditional RPG. But within that traditional framework, it transcends it, making the player an integral part of the game. You will be moved and touched. Ultimately, you will gain hope and be filled with determination. This game creates a complete worldview, where we experience unique adventures; in reality, we create our own worlds, driven by emotion or faith. Those virtual memes, those fabricated anomalies, become the driving force propelling us forward in reality, thus transcending the boundaries between virtual and reality.
Comment3: Sunflower Game Rating: 8/10 (1-10) This is a revolutionary pixel-art role-playing game where players take on the role of a human who accidentally falls into an underground world. Combat is turn-based, but innovatively incorporates light bullet hell elements. The most groundbreaking aspect is that players can choose to defeat monsters to gain experience or befriend them. The game includes numerous easter eggs, a rich dialogue system, and a captivating soundtrack. Achieving the perfect ending brings a level of reflection on humanity and emotion unmatched by other games; players will realize that this is not just a game, but a work of art.
Comment4: As IGN stated, there are no perfect games. Those masterpieces that receive a perfect score of 10 embody the expectations of IGN editors for a perfect game, and they will influence game design for years to come. Undertale proves that art style does not affect character development. No AAA game can provide the same narrative experience, meta elements, and character soul as Undertale, which reached unprecedented heights. To this day, there is no substitute for Undertale, and there may never be one.
